timeline.label: Q1 2026 earnings: revenue +1.3% YoY, operating income +12% YoY; full-year 2026 guidance reiterated
FY2025 10-K filed: $24.9B revenue, $4.6B operating income, $2.3B operating cash flow; three-segment structure post-Health Care separation
FY2025 repurchased ~$3.3B of common stock while paying $3.3B dividends; share count down to ~530M
Solventum (Health Care) spin-off completed; 3M retained three segments: Safety and Industrial, Transportation and Electronics, Consumer
Public water suppliers PFAS class-action settlement framework; ongoing litigation accruals and cash payments
Announced Solventum spin-off of Health Care business and commitment to exit PFAS manufacturing by end of 2025
Committed to exit PFAS manufacturing and discontinue PFAS use across product portfolio by end of 2025
Mike Roman CEO era: portfolio simplification, litigation management, and spin execution

industry.structure: Global diversified industrials with scale R&D, manufacturing, and distribution. Post-Solventum, 3M competes in safety/industrial abrasives and PPE, electronics materials, and consumer brands. Concentrated share in niche SKUs but broad competition in each category.
industry.share_shift: 3M regaining operating momentum in Safety and Industrial (+3.9% revenue, +14% operating income FY2025) while Transportation and Electronics revenue declined slightly; Consumer flat. PFAS exit removes a liability overhang but also former product lines.
industry.trend: Portfolio simplification (Health Care spin, PFAS exit), litigation settlement cadence, and industrial end-market normalization. Buybacks and dividends remain large relative to reported free cash flow.
